sanskrit:
न वेदयज्ञाध्ययनैर्न दानैर्न च क्रियाभिर्न तपोभिरुग्रैः।
एवंरूपः शक्य अहं नृलोके द्रष्टुं त्वदन्येन कुरुप्रवीर॥
transliteration: na veda-yajñādhyayanair na dānair na ca kriyābhir na tapobhir ugraiḥ / evaṃ-rūpaḥ śakya ahaṃ nṛ-loke draṣṭuṃ tvad-anyena kuru-pravīra
literal_meaning: Not by Vedic study, sacrifices, charity, pious works, or fierce austerities can this form of Mine be seen in the human world by anyone other than you, O best of the Kurus.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna is told that the terrifying cosmic vision he is witnessing is inaccessible through conventional religious or moral efforts; it is granted solely by divine grace to a surrendered heart.
psychological_state: Awe-induced ego dissolution: the self-concept that relied on spiritual accomplishments is shattered, leaving raw humility and privileged intimacy.
trigger_scenario: A high-achieving professional receives a life-changing medical diagnosis despite perfect health habits, realizing that control is an illusion and grace is primary.
anchor_text: God’s true form is revealed only by grace, not rituals.
